'''Jimmy Smith''' (born 25 October 1971) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|sr}} and {{rlp|pr}} in the 1990s and 2000s.

He played for the [[Sydney Roosters|Eastern Suburbs]], the [[Western Suburbs Magpies|Western Suburbs]] and the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] in the [[Winfield Cup]], [[Australian Rugby League|ARL]] and [[NRL]]. He also played in the [[Super League]] for the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]].

==Background==
Smith was born in [[Young, New South Wales]].

==Career==
After his retirement from playing Smith was kept on by the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs|Rabbitohs]] in the role of media liaison officer.<ref name="Inspiring People">{{cite web|url=http://www.celebrityspeakers.com.au/jimmy-smith/|title=Inspiring People|publisher=Celebrity Speakers|access-date=24 December 2016}}</ref>

Smith has since moved into the media and works for [[Fox Sports (Australia)#Rugby league|Fox Sports]] as a pundit and commentator.<ref name="Warriors denied try of the season by bunker blunder">{{cite web|url=http://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/warriors-denied-try-of-the-season-by-bunker-blunder/news-story/080cbe1ed66fb09bf84c211999ef73c6|title=Warriors denied try of the season by bunker blunder|publisher=News|date=29 August 2016|access-date=24 December 2016}}</ref> He also works in radio for SEN 1170 Sydney, hosting the show “Afternoons with Jimmy Smith” from 12-3 pm Monday to Friday. Jimmy also commentates on numerous sporting events across the SEN Network, from NRL, AFL, Supercars, to NBL as well.<ref name="SMITHS MOVING ON UP">{{cite web|url=http://www.domain.com.au/news/former-sports-stars-on-the-move-20150716-gidutt/|title=SMITHS MOVING ON UP|publisher=Celebrity Speakers|date=18 July 2015|access-date=24 December 2016}}</ref>

Smith studied for an Agricultural Economics degree at [[Sydney University]].<ref name="Jimmy Smith">{{cite web|url=http://www.saxton.com.au/jimmy-smith/|title=Jimmy Smith|publisher=Saxton|access-date=24 December 2016}}</ref>
